concerns.text: T024 hard-codes the sensitivity sweep values to the discrete set {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}. The spec (FR-004) requires sweeping 'over a range of small magnitudes' to report variation. Without a defined range or step logic in the task, an implementer cannot deterministically execute this; they must guess the range. The task must specify the range (e.g., 0.0 to 0.2 in steps of 0.01) or the logic to derive it.

T023 requires calculating accuracy on the 'top 10' PCMs and verifying '≥ 60% accuracy'. However, the spec (SC-003) explicitly defers the 'top [deferred]' count. The task hard-codes '10' without a mechanism to determine this value (e.g., 'determine N via Phase 0 analysis' or 'use N=10 if Phase 0 confirms'). This makes the success criterion ambiguous if the research phase determines a different N is appropriate.

T013b implements a fallback if 'overlap < 500'. The spec (US-1, Scenario 3) states the fallback triggers if 'NIST overlap is < 500 compounds' (referring to training data proxy validation), but T013b applies this to the 'external validation set' (literature PCMs). The spec requires the external set to be 50 PCMs (Constitution VII). An implementer cannot execute this deterministically because the threshold (500) contradicts the known size of the external set (50), creating a logical impossibility or ambiguity on which dataset to check.

T001 lists directory creation but includes 'specs/contracts' which conflicts with the plan's structure (specs/001-.../contracts/). The task should explicitly reference the correct relative path per the plan.md structure to ensure the implementer creates the directory in the right place.

Tasks T026a-c require generating report sections in 'research.md' and 'paper/'. The spec defines success criteria (SC-001, SC-002, SC-003) with '[deferred]' values. These tasks do not specify how to handle the deferred values (e.g., 'insert the measured value from the previous step' or 'leave as [deferred] if not yet computed'). Without this, the implementer cannot deterministically complete the report generation.
